sn5.6:1.2: Then, early in the morning, Cālā the nun adjusted her robes and, taking her bowl & outer robe, went into Sāvatthī for alms. When she had gone for alms in Sāvatthī and had returned from her alms round, after her meal she went to the Grove of the Blind to spend the day.
sn5.6:1.3: Having gone deep into the Grove of the Blind, she sat down at the foot of a tree for the day’s abiding.
sn5.6:1.4: Then Māra the Evil One, wanting to arouse fear, horripilation, & terror in her, wanting to make her fall away from concentration, approached her & said,
sn5.6:1.5: “What is it that you don’t approve of, nun?”
sn5.6:1.6: “I don’t approve of birth, my friend.”
sn5.6:2.1: Māra: “Why don’t you approve of birth?
sn5.6:2.2: One who is born enjoys sensual pleasures.
sn5.6:2.3: Who on earth ever persuaded you:
sn5.6:3.1: Sister Cālā: “For one who is born there’s death.
sn5.6:3.2: One who is born sees pain.
sn5.6:3.3: It’s a binding, a flogging, a torment.
sn5.6:3.4: That’s why one shouldn’t approve of birth.
sn5.6:4.1: The Awakened One taught me the Dhamma
sn5.6:4.3: for the abandoning of all pain,
sn5.6:4.4: he established me in the truth.
sn5.6:5.1: But beings who have come to form
sn5.6:5.2: & those with a share in the formless,
sn5.6:6.1: Then Māra the Evil One—sad & dejected at realizing, “Cālā the nun knows me”—vanished right there.
